tech_name: Intel Xeon Processor with Intel EM64T (Extended Memory 64 Technology)
notes: Primary subject of white paper; trigger for broad 64-bit migration; replaces pure 32-bit x86
High-end 64-bit for business-critical data tier; EPIC architecture; market from $1B (2003) to $8B (2008) IDC forecast
Being displaced by EM64T; 4GB memory limit; performance reference baseline
Intel EM64T is Intel's implementation; IDC: 32-bit x86 designs to be swept away over time
Natural migration of IA-32 to 64-bit; 64-bit pointers/registers, additional SSE registers, 64-bit integer support
Explicitly Parallel Instruction Computing (EPIC) based; business-critical enterprise/HPC
One of five OS options on Itanium architecture; 64-bit Linux support for EM64T
64-bit Windows Server support for Itanium and EM64T platforms
